channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
item.description: Researchers have identified dozens of malicious GlassWorm extensions that come with new evasion techniques....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
item.description: Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Olympics from evolving threats....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GlassWorm Attack Uses Stolen GitHub Tokens to Force-Push Malware Into Python Repos
item.description: The GlassWorm malware campaign is being used to fuel an ongoing attack that leverages the stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. "The attack targets Python projec...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stryker attack wiped tens of thousands of devices, no malware needed
item.description: Last week's cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker was limited to its internal Microsoft environment and remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA flags Wing FTP Server flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA warned U.S. government agencies to secure their Wing FTP Server instances against an actively exploited vulnerability that may be chained in remote code execution attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UK’s Companies House confirms security flaw exposed business data
item.description: Companies House, a British government agency that operates the registry for all U.K. companies, says its WebFiling service is back online after it was closed on Friday to fix a security flaw that expo...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Online outage blocks access to mailboxes
item.description: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing Exchange Online outage that is preventing customers from accessing their mailboxes and calendars. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Days, Router Botnets, AWS Breach, Rogue AI Agents & More
item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting sharper, an...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
item.description: A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Shadow AI is everywhere. Here’s how to find and secure it.
item.description: Shadow AI is quietly spreading across SaaS environments as employees adopt new AI tools without IT oversight. Nudge Security explains how security teams can discover AI apps, monitor usage, and govern...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft pulls Samsung app blocking Windows C: drive from Store
item.description: ​Microsoft has removed the Samsung Galaxy Connect app from the Microsoft Store because it was causing issues on specific Samsung Galaxy Book 4 and desktop models running Windows 11. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Why Security Validation Is Becoming Agentic
item.description: If you run security at any reasonably complex organization, your validation stack probably looks something like this: a BAS tool in one corner. A pentest engagement, or maybe an automated pentesting p...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Spread MacSync macOS Infostealer via Fake AI Tool Installers
item.description: Three different ClickFix campaigns have been found to act as a delivery vector for the deployment of a macOS information stealer called MacSync. "Unlike traditional exploit-based attacks, this method ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: DRILLAPP Backdoor Targets Ukraine, Abuses Microsoft Edge Debugging for Stealth Espionage
item.description: Ukrainian entities have emerged as the target of a new campaign likely orchestrated by threat actors linked to Russia, according to a report from S2 Grupo's LAB52 threat intelligence team. The campaig...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Android 17 Blocks Non-Accessibility Apps from Accessibility API to Prevent Malware Abuse
item.description: Google is testing a new security feature as part of Android Advanced Protection Mode (AAPM) that prevents certain kinds of apps from using the accessibility services API. The change, incorporated in A...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI says ChatGPT ads are not rolling out globally for now
item.description: OpenAI told BleepingComputer that ChatGPT ads on Free and Go plans are not yet rolling out outside the United States, even though some users noticed references to ads in the updated privacy policy. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Betterleaks, a new open-source secrets scanner to replace Gitleaks
item.description: A new open-source tool called Betterleaks can scan directories, files, and git repositories and identify valid secrets using default or customized rules. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 11 OOB hotpatch to fix RRAS RCE flaw
item.description: Microsoft has released an out-of-band (OOB) update to fix a security vulnerabilities affecting Windows 11 Enterprise devices that receive hotpatch updates instead of the regular Patch Tuesday cumulati...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenClaw AI Agent Flaws Could Enable Prompt Injection and Data Exfiltration
item.description: China's National Computer Network Emergency Response Technical Team (CNCERT) has issued a warning about the security stemming from the use of OpenClaw (formerly Clawdbot and Moltbot), an open-source a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AppsFlyer Web SDK hijacked to spread crypto-stealing JavaScript code
item.description: The AppsFlyer Web SDK was temporarily hijacked this week with malicious code used to steal cryptocurrency in a supply-chain attack. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: GlassWorm Supply-Chain Attack Abuses 72 Open VSX Extensions to Target Developers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new iteration of the GlassWorm campaign that they say represents a "significant escalation" in how it propagates through the Open VSX registry. "Instead of req...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft: Windows 11 users can't access C: drive on some Samsung PCs
item.description: Microsoft is investigating a new issue affecting some Samsung laptops running Windows 11 after installing the February 2026 security updates, in which users lose access to their C:\ drive and are unab...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI seeks victims of Steam games used to spread malware
item.description: The FBI is asking gamers who installed Steam titles containing malware to provide information as part of an ongoing investigation into eight malicious games uploaded to the gaming platform. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake PoCs, Misunderstood Risks Cause Cisco SD-WAN Chaos
item.description: The excitement around Cisco's latest SD-WAN bugs has inspired some light fraud, misunderstandings, and overlooked potential hazards....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Data Gap: Why Nonprofit Cyber Incidents Go Underreported
item.description: Threat actors target nonprofits due to security gaps and highly coveted information, but a lack of sufficient data makes it difficult to grasp the entire picture....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks Unit 42...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cyberattackers Don't Care About Good Causes
item.description: Sightline Security's founder and advisory board discuss how cybersecurity poses significant problems for nonprofits and suggest ways the industry can help....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Poland's nuclear research centre targeted by cyberattack
item.description: Poland's National Centre for Nuclear Research (NCBJ) says hackers targeted its IT infrastructure, but the attack was detected and blocked before causing any impact. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Meta to Shut Down Instagram End-to-End Encrypted Chat Support Starting May 2026
item.description: Meta has announced plans to discontinue support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for chats on Instagram after May 8, 2026. "If you have chats that are impacted by this change, you will see instruction...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft investigates classic Outlook sync and connection issues
item.description: ​Microsoft is investigating several issues causing email synchronization and connection problems when using the classic Outlook desktop client. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
